topics.name: Introduction to Literature and Literary Appreciation
topics.description: Literary genres, literary devices, figures of speech, poetic devices, and analytical techniques.

subtopics.name: Literary Genres and Concepts
objectives: Distinguish Prose, Poetry, and Drama genres and sub-genres
Identify plot, theme, characterization, setting, point of view, and atmosphere

subtopics.name: Figures of Speech and Poetic Devices
objectives: Identify and interpret simile, metaphor, personification, irony, oxymoron, hyperbole, paradox, symbolism
Analyze rhyme scheme, rhythm, meter, stanza, alliteration, assonance, and imagery in poetry

topics.name: African and Non-African Prose
topics.description: Critical analysis of set African and Non-African novels, themes, characters, and stylistic devices.

subtopics.name: African Prose Analysis
objectives: Analyze plot summary, character roles, socio-cultural background, and themes in prescribed African novels
Evaluate narrative techniques, symbolism, and linguistic style in African prose

subtopics.name: Non-African Prose Analysis
objectives: Analyze plot, themes, characterization, and historical context in prescribed Non-African novels

topics.name: African and Non-African Drama and Poetry
topics.description: Critical analysis of set African/Non-African plays (tragedy, comedy) and prescribed poems.

subtopics.name: African and Non-African Drama
objectives: Analyze dramatic structure, acts, scenes, conflict, dialogue, monologue, soliloquy, and dramatic irony in prescribed plays

subtopics.name: African and Non-African Poetry
objectives: Analyze theme, tone, mood, structure, imagery, and poetic devices in prescribed African and Non-African poems
